codeReview.summary: The fix removes explicit profile-ID launch overrides by adding launchUsingActiveProfile() and adds rapid-switch tests, but the new assertions still do not prove routed profile ID equality to the final committed active profile for browser/CLI same-type switch chains.

issues.description: The A->B->C browser active-state contract test uses three Chrome profiles (lines 994/1001/1008) but only asserts .browserNotInstalled(.chrome). Because all candidates share the same browser type and the unavailable-provider path fails before profile-specific launch args are built, stale routing to A or B would still pass. This does not prove the routed profile ID equals the final committed active profile ID.

The CLI A->B->C active-state test uses codexA/codexB/codexC (lines 1257/1266/1275) but validates only executable type (.codex). A stale route to codexA or codexB still satisfies the assertion, so the test does not establish that launch consumed the final committed active profile ID (codexC).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked followedProcedure=true without recording the required context-read evidence for that claim.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md requires concrete context-read command evidence when followedProcedure=true, but handoff 2026-04-10T03-26-39-045Z.../c9f00843... lists only xcodebuild build-for-testing and xcodebuild test commands.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill's validation-stack expectations are not consistently enforced in handoffs marked as procedure-followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md step 5 expects swift package tests + scoped xcodebuild + scripts/test-openburnbar-app.sh + smoke checks when relevant; this handoff reports only two xcodebuild commands despite followedProcedure=true.

summary: Fail: this fix correctly introduces active-state launch entrypoints and removes explicit profile-ID overrides, but the new rapid-switch assertions remain type-level and do not prove final committed profile-ID routing for same-type chains, so the prior launch-routing failure is not fully resolved.
